changes_description: Added Phase 2 polish loop (perturb around current best + re-optimize) and an extra restart at scale 0.12 with tighter ftol=1e-11 and maxiter=500, matching the structure of the top-scoring Program 2 (0.9971) which strictly dominates the parent (0.9963).

metadata.changes: Added Phase 2 polish loop (perturb around current best + re-optimize) and an extra restart at scale 0.12 with tighter ft

blocks.text: Program 2 is the stronger choice here—it achieved a higher score of 0.9971 while being more efficient than Program 1, so I'll use its structure as the foundation for the child submission.

tool_input.changes_description: Added Phase 2 polish loop (perturb around current best + re-optimize) and an extra restart at scale 0.12 with tighter ftol=1e-11 and maxiter=500, matching the structure of the top-scoring Program 2 (0.9971) which strictly dominates the parent (0.9963).

summary.approach: Added Phase 2 polish loop (5 perturbation scales around best solution) and extra restart at scale 0.12, matching top-scoring Program 2 structure with ftol=1e-11 and maxiter=500
summary.key_insight: Perturbing around the current best solution in a dedicated polish phase (Phase 2) with small scales (0.003–0.04) consistently improves sum_radii beyond what Phase 1 hex-lattice restarts alone achieve
next_directions: Try seeding from known near-optimal Packomania coordinates for n=26 as an alternative initial condition to the hex lattice, which may land in a better basin of attraction
Add a Phase 3 that specifically perturbs only the smallest-radius circles while keeping large circles fixed, targeting the weakest links in the packing
Increase Phase 2 polish iterations (more scales or repeated rounds) or use a tighter shrink factor (0.97 instead of 0.85) to start SLSQP closer to the current optimum
avoid: Single-start SLSQP from hex lattice without perturbation (score ~0.9929, strictly dominated)
Multi-pass polish with 3 rounds and Phase 3 targeting 10 smallest circles (Attempt 3, score 0.9969, worse than simpler Phase 2 polish)

reps_annotations.hypothesis: 5x5+1 grid warm start + SLSQP with analytical per-constraint Jacobians (ftol=1e-11, maxiter=500) reliably achieves the 0.999956 plateau
